products.description: Broad-spectrum botanical insecticide with antifeedant, repellent, and IGR properties for sucking and chewing pest management.
Naturally derived insecticide with contact and ingestion activity against lepidopteran pests and thrips.
Entomopathogenic fungal biocontrol agent for management of sucking pests, borers, and white grubs.
Soil-applied biocontrol fungus for suppression of soil-borne diseases including root rot, wilt, and damping off.
Plant growth enhancer derived from seaweed that improves stress tolerance, root development, and overall crop vigor.
Multi-component biostimulant that enhances nutrient uptake, soil biology, and plant resilience against abiotic stress.
Zinc-solubilizing microbial biofertilizer that improves zinc availability and uptake in zinc-deficient soils.

packages.name: Rice Borer Management Pack
packages.objective: Integrated management of stem borers in rice through biological control and plant strengthening
packages.dosageNote: Apply Spindura Plus at 2 ml/L as foliar spray at first sign of dead hearts. Follow with Mycova at 2.5 g/L for residual biological control. Support with Blooma at 2 ml/L for crop recovery.
packages.complianceNote: All components are approved for use in organic and integrated pest management programs.

packages.name: Rice Blast Shield Pack
packages.objective: Preventive and curative management of blast disease in rice using biological agents
packages.dosageNote: Apply Biota-V at 2.5 g/L as soil drench before transplanting. Spray Mycova at 2.5 g/L at first symptom appearance. Support with Envicta at 2 ml/L for enhanced plant defense.
packages.complianceNote: Suitable for organic cultivation and IPM programs. No pre-harvest interval required.

packages.name: Rice Zinc Nutrition Pack
packages.objective: Correction and prevention of zinc deficiency in rice through microbial and biostimulant support
packages.dosageNote: Apply IGreen Zn at 2.5 g/L as soil application at transplanting. Follow with Envicta at 2 ml/L and Blooma at 2 ml/L as foliar sprays at tillering.
packages.complianceNote: All components are biological. Safe for organic production systems.

packages.name: Cotton Whitefly Management Pack
packages.objective: Integrated biological management of whitefly in cotton during vegetative growth
packages.dosageNote: Spray Ecoza Max at 2-3 ml/L at first sighting of whitefly. Follow with Mycova at 2.5 g/L after 7 days for sustained biological control. Apply Blooma at 2 ml/L to restore crop vigor.
packages.complianceNote: Compatible with IPM programs. No harmful residues on lint or seed.

packages.name: Soil Health Foundation Pack
packages.objective: Establish healthy soil biology and improve nutrient availability at planting

productRoles.role: Soil disease suppression and root zone protection
Soil organic matter and microbial activity enhancement

packages.dosageNote: Mix Biota-V at 2.5 g/L and IGreen Zn at 2.5 g/L for soil application at planting. Follow with Envicta at 2 ml/L as drench after 15 days.
packages.complianceNote: All biological inputs. Suitable for organic certification programs.

packages.name: Abiotic Stress Shield Pack
packages.objective: Improve crop resilience against drought, heat, and transplant shock through biostimulant support
packages.dosageNote: Apply Blooma at 2-3 ml/L as preventive foliar spray before anticipated stress. Follow with Envicta at 2 ml/L to enhance root recovery and nutrient access.
packages.complianceNote: Plant-based biostimulants with no chemical residue concerns.

recommendations.recommendationReason: Complete biological borer management combining Spinosad-based control, entomopathogenic reinforcement, and crop recovery support — ideal for Kharif rice during tillering.
recommendations.dosageNote: Apply Spindura Plus at 2 ml/L at dead heart stage. Follow with Mycova at 2.5 g/L. Support with Blooma at 2 ml/L.
recommendations.regionRelevance: Recommended for South and East India rice belts
recommendations.complianceNote: All components approved for organic and IPM use.

recommendations.recommendationReason: Spinosad-based botanical insecticide with proven efficacy against rice stem borers through contact and ingestion activity.
recommendations.dosageNote: 2 ml/L foliar spray at first sign of dead hearts. Repeat after 10-14 days if infestation persists.

recommendations.recommendationReason: Integrated biological blast management using Trichoderma for soil-level suppression, Beauveria for foliar support, and biostimulant-based plant strengthening.
recommendations.dosageNote: Biota-V at 2.5 g/L soil drench. Mycova at 2.5 g/L foliar. Envicta at 2 ml/L for defense enhancement.
recommendations.regionRelevance: South India and East India blast-prone zones
recommendations.complianceNote: No PHI required. Safe for organic programs.

recommendations.recommendationReason: Trichoderma viride provides competitive colonization of the root zone and suppresses Magnaporthe oryzae through mycoparasitism and induced systemic resistance.
recommendations.dosageNote: 2.5 g/L as soil drench before transplanting or at early tillering.

recommendations.recommendationReason: Targeted zinc nutrition program using zinc-solubilizing microbes, humic-fulvic uptake enhancement, and seaweed-based root stimulation to correct zinc deficiency.
recommendations.dosageNote: IGreen Zn at 2.5 g/L soil application. Envicta at 2 ml/L and Blooma at 2 ml/L as foliar support.
recommendations.regionRelevance: Zinc-deficient soils across all rice belts
recommendations.complianceNote: All biological inputs. Suitable for organic certification.

recommendations.recommendationReason: Zinc-solubilizing microbial consortium that converts insoluble soil zinc into plant-available forms, addressing deficiency at the root zone level.
recommendations.dosageNote: 2.5 g/L as soil application at transplanting. Can be mixed with organic matter for basal application.
recommendations.complianceNote: Microbial biofertilizer. Safe for organic systems.

recommendations.recommendationReason: Integrated whitefly management combining neem-based antifeedant action, entomopathogenic fungal control, and biostimulant-based crop recovery for vegetative cotton.
recommendations.dosageNote: Ecoza Max at 2-3 ml/L first spray. Mycova at 2.5 g/L after 7 days. Blooma at 2 ml/L for vigor.
recommendations.regionRelevance: Central India and South India cotton zones
recommendations.complianceNote: IPM-compatible. No harmful residues on cotton lint.

recommendations.recommendationReason: Azadirachtin-based botanical insecticide with strong antifeedant and insect growth regulator properties effective against whitefly nymphs and adults.
recommendations.dosageNote: 2-3 ml/L foliar spray. Repeat every 7-10 days during active infestation.
recommendations.complianceNote: Botanical pesticide. Approved for organic cotton programs.

recommendations.recommendationReason: Biostimulant-based stress management combining seaweed extract for hormonal balance and humic-fulvic complex for root resilience and nutrient mobilization.
recommendations.dosageNote: Blooma at 2-3 ml/L preventive spray. Envicta at 2 ml/L for root and nutrient support.
recommendations.stageRelevance: All growth stages — especially critical at transplanting and flowering
recommendations.seasonalRelevance: All seasons — especially important during dry spells
recommendations.complianceNote: Plant-based biostimulants. No chemical residue concerns.

recommendations.recommendationReason: Seaweed-based biostimulant that enhances drought and heat tolerance through natural cytokinin and betaine content, supporting osmotic adjustment and cell protection.
recommendations.dosageNote: 2-3 ml/L foliar spray. Apply 3-5 days before anticipated stress event for best results.
recommendations.seasonalRelevance: Summer and dry spells across seasons
recommendations.regionRelevance: All regions — priority in rain-fed agriculture

recommendations.recommendationReason: Trichoderma viride is the primary biological agent for soil-borne disease suppression across horticultural crops. It provides competitive root zone colonization and induced plant defense.
recommendations.dosageNote: 2.5 g/L as soil drench at planting or transplanting. Repeat at 30-day intervals for sustained protection.
recommendations.seasonalRelevance: All seasons — critical during humid conditions
recommendations.complianceNote: Biological fungal agent. No chemical residue.

recommendations.recommendationReason: Premium seaweed-based biostimulant that enhances overall crop performance through improved root development, stress tolerance, and nutrient efficiency.
recommendations.dosageNote: 2 ml/L foliar spray at key growth stages. Compatible with most spray programs.
recommendations.stageRelevance: All growth stages — especially vegetative and flowering
recommendations.complianceNote: Natural seaweed extract. Certified for organic use.

recommendations.recommendationReason: Multi-component biostimulant combining humic acid, fulvic acid, and amino acids to enhance soil biology, nutrient uptake, and plant resilience.
recommendations.dosageNote: 2 ml/L foliar or soil application. Best results with repeated applications at 15-day intervals.
recommendations.regionRelevance: All regions — priority in degraded or low-organic-matter soils

recommendations.recommendationReason: Microbial consortium-based biofertilizer that solubilizes zinc and improves micronutrient availability in the root zone, supporting healthier crop growth.
recommendations.dosageNote: 2.5 g/L soil application at planting. Can be combined with organic manure for enhanced efficacy.
recommendations.complianceNote: Microbial biofertilizer suitable for organic farming.

recommendations.recommendationReason: Trichoderma viride provides effective biocontrol against Rhizoctonia solani through direct mycoparasitism and competitive root zone colonization in rice.
recommendations.dosageNote: 2.5 g/L soil drench at transplanting. Foliar spray at 2.5 g/L at booting if symptoms appear.
recommendations.regionRelevance: East India and South India lowland rice areas

recommendations.recommendationReason: Azadirachtin-based broad-spectrum botanical insecticide effective against multiple sucking pest species through antifeedant, repellent, and growth-disrupting modes of action.
recommendations.dosageNote: 2-3 ml/L foliar spray. Apply in the evening for best results. Repeat every 7-10 days.
recommendations.seasonalRelevance: All seasons — peak demand during warm humid weather
recommendations.complianceNote: Botanical pesticide. Approved for organic and IPM.

recommendations.recommendationReason: Spinosad-based natural insecticide with strong contact and ingestion activity against lepidopteran caterpillar pests including Spodoptera, bollworms, and fruit borers.
recommendations.dosageNote: 2 ml/L foliar spray targeting early larval instars. Repeat after 10-14 days if needed.
recommendations.seasonalRelevance: All seasons — critical during pest peak periods
recommendations.complianceNote: Naturally derived insecticide. Approved for organic use.

recommendations.recommendationReason: Foundation soil health program to establish healthy root zones at planting — combines disease suppression, zinc solubilization, and organic matter enhancement.
recommendations.dosageNote: Biota-V + IGreen Zn at 2.5 g/L each as soil mix. Envicta at 2 ml/L as drench at 15 days.
recommendations.regionRelevance: All regions — priority in degraded soils
